findings.evidence: `rg -n "fiberMapStabilizerEquiv|subgroupMap" code/TauCeti/AlgebraicTopology/UniversalCover/Deck/FiberTransport.lean` shows `@[expose] def fiberMapStabilizerEquiv` at line 183 and its implementation through `subgroupMap` at line 187.
findings.fix: Keep the equivalence body hidden and export characteristic lemmas instead; if the current `rfl` proof forces exposure, rework the definition/API rather than exposing this wrapper body.
findings.issue: `fiberMapStabilizerEquiv` is marked `@[expose]`, exposing the `subgroupMap`/`subgroupCongr` construction as public interface.

findings.evidence: `rg -n "fiberMapStabilizerEquiv.*symm|fiberMapStabilizerEquiv_apply_coe" code/TauCeti/AlgebraicTopology/UniversalCover/Deck/FiberTransport.lean` finds the forward lemma at line 208 and no corresponding `symm` lemma; Mathlib's analogous `stabilizerEquivStabilizer` has both `_apply` and `_symm_apply`.
findings.fix: Add a simp lemma characterizing `((fiberMapStabilizerEquiv h hpq e).symm ψ : Deck p)` as `(conjMulEquiv h hpq).symm ψ.1`.
findings.issue: `fiberMapStabilizerEquiv` has only the forward coercion characterization; users transporting a target stabilizer back have no `symm` coercion lemma.

summary: The stabilizer transport API still exposes an implementation wrapper and only characterizes the forward direction of the equivalence.
